evidence_quotes.quote: hundred games not accessible anymore just because they are structured to be "temporary" despite hundred of gamers bought them.
blatant abuses and legal quibbles on corporations' EULAs.

paragraphs.text: Hi, I'd really like the Digital Fairness Act to be understood and discussed accordingly as our rights as consumers should NOT be ignored by blatant abuses and legal quibbles on corporations' EULAs. We already have a list of hundred games not accessible anymore just because they are structured to be "temporary" despite hundred of gamers bought them. We should address immediately as greedy corporations like Ubisoft are gaslighting us gamers because they have the knife by the handle and they JUST CAN do it by their own (for now legal and hopefully not for much longer) rules. This will effect everyone's life and online communities health as this will reduce frustration and overall negativity against the industry, the corporations and the istitutions (obviously resulting in illegal acts like piracy). The gaming industry is blooming and trying to ignore it and let it collapse on itself will not benefit anyone. Piracy (yes, piracy ALSO a consequence of this) is A SERVICE PROBLEM and until the agreements and services will be disrespectul against the consumers, the problem will never attenuate and will grow more. Give consumers the rightful respect they deserve and everyone (if in good faith) will benefit from it. YOU are our last chance to change things and milions of players are asking to be heard. Thank you.
